Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 286 upvotes, 51 comments
  2. The 185-Microsecond Type Hint 61 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. The Cathode Ray Tube site 28 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Daily Driving GrapheneOS 20 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: Visual Lambda Calculus – a thesis project (2008) revived for the web 39 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 310 upvotes, 83 comments
  7. RCade: Building a Community Arcade Cabinet 68 upvotes, 14 comments
  8. Simple screw counter 63 upvotes, 13 comments
  9. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 309 upvotes, 92 comments
  10. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 2134 upvotes, 772 comments
  11. Seed of Might Color Correction Process (2023) [pdf] 82 upvotes, 20 comments
  12. Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source 75 upvotes, 18 comments
  13. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 94 upvotes, 32 comments
  14. Show HN: Govbase – Follow a bill from source text to news bias to social posts 181 upvotes, 74 comments
  15. British Columbia is permanently adopting daylight time 673 upvotes, 336 comments
  16. The Excommunicated Devs Making Games with AI 27 upvotes, 7 comments
  17. Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes 162 upvotes, 74 comments
  18. Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns 902 upvotes, 516 comments
  19. Buckle Up for Bumpier Skies 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. Moldova broke our data pipeline 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Programmable Cryptography (2024) 62 upvotes, 35 comments
  22. Welcome (back) to Macintosh 292 upvotes, 209 comments
  23. Against Query Based Compilers 51 upvotes, 28 comments
  24. Parallel coding agents with tmux and Markdown specs 141 upvotes, 109 comments
  25. Intent-Based Commits 23 upvotes, 13 comments
  26. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 192 upvotes, 235 comments
  27. iPhone 17e 244 upvotes, 338 comments
  28. Guilty Displeasures 46 upvotes, 53 comments
  29. New iPad Air, powered by M4 361 upvotes, 576 comments
  30. Ask HN: Who wants to be hired? (March 2026) 88 upvotes, 211 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 3 March 2026. Source here.
